rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' Unverified First Amended Complaint For Damages
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. DEFENDANT FRYER-KNOWLES, INC.¿s motion to strike punitive damages is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint sufficiently alleges a claim for punitive damages and sets forth the facts upon which the claim is premised. Plaintiffs allege that Fryer-Knowles has known of the health hazards of asbestos since at least the 1930s and concealed information regarding these hazards from the general public. Plaintiffs specifically allege that Fryer-Knowles suppressed the ¿Lanza Report¿ and influenced the author to change his report, thereby concealing information from Plaintiff and other users. Plaintiffs further allege that Fryer-Knowles¿s officers, directors, and managing agents had knowledge of and authorized the allegedly fraudulent, oppressive, and malicious acts. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY GLIDEWELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint That Refer To Plaintiff'S Employment By Tosco At Tosco Refinery
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. DEFENDANT TOSCO REFINING COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint That Refer To Plaintiff'S Employment By Tosco At Tosco Refinery. In light of the permission given to plaintiff to file an amendement to the complaint, Tosco¿s motion to strike is moot. One Order for demurrer and motion to strike was signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Scott Hughes, CSR #12365

rulings.Case Title: GARY GLIDEWELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS, et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, January 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. DEFENDANT TOSCO REFINING COMPANY, INC.¿s demurer to the twenty-second cause of action for civil battery is moot because plaintiff has dismissed that claim against Tosco. Tosco¿s demurrer to the balance of the complaint is sustained and plaintiff¿s request for leave to file an amendment to the complaint is granted. =(220/HEK)
